action.reasoning: Observation: Training crashed with ValueError: Input contains NaN in sklearn average_precision_score (on y_score/preds) after pos weights and data load; previous best val AP ~0.1708. No jsonl metrics so curves uninformative. History shows unmasked NaNs in labels for BCE loss (causing NaN gradients/weights/outputs), incomplete valid=~np.i in val func, and scheduler/verbose issues before. Analysis: Root cause is lack of is_labeled masking in loss (NaNs propagate), no nan_to_num/try-except in AP per-task, no grad clipping (exploding grads with pos_weight+imbalance). FP+MLP baseline is stable but can be improved with BN for stability, lower dropout, more epochs, proper logging. PyG still avoided due to past segfaults. Decision: Update model.py (add BN1d + dropout=0.1 for stability/conv), fully rewrite train.py (masked loss, robust per-task AP with nan handling, grad clip, ReduceLROnPlateau, per-epoch jsonl logging, 20 epochs, best.pt on val_ap), keep predict.py; run training then submit_eval on best.pt for fixed pipeline + improved avg_precision.
